rationale: Keep: the citing paper’s core contribution is a household-level decomposition of promotion effects into brand switching, stockpiling, and consumption, and the cited paper is directly about how to interpret/decompose the promotion bump ("75%" vs "33%" switching) in the same scanner-panel context. Given this tight construct overlap and that the citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior promotion-decomposition work, this is plausibly a substantive methodological/conceptual reference rather than an incidental mention.
